article_title: Chinese Academy of Sciences Reports All-Iron Flow Battery Running 6,000 Cycles With No Capacity Decay and 99.4% Coulombic Efficiency
summary: CAS all-iron flow battery — every numeric and named claim verified against 5 reachable snapshots; Wiley journal abstract bot-blocked is non-substantive (journal cited only for paper existence). One subordinate misattribution: a CleanTechnica passage attributed to CleanTechnica directly is actually CleanTechnica quoting News 18.

content_preview.title: Chinese Academy of Sciences Reports All-Iron Flow Battery Running 6,000 Cycles With No Capacity Decay and 99.4% Coulombic Efficiency
content_preview.summary: A team at the Institute of Metal Research engineered a new iron-complex electrolyte that ran 6,000 cycles at 80 mA per square centimeter with no capacity decay and a 99.4 percent average coulombic efficiency, opening a possible low-cost route to long-duration grid storage.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A research team at the Chinese Academy of Sciences has reported an alkaline all-iron flow battery that ran for more than 6,000 charge-discharge cycles at 80 milliamps per square centimeter without measurable capacity decay, according to a press release from the [Chinese Academy of Sciences](https://english.cas.cn/newsroom/research-news/202604/t20260430_1158284.shtml) describing work published in the journal Advanced Energy Materials. The result, the agency said, addresses a long-sta...

recommendations: Add english.cas.cn, e.vnexpress.net, advanced.onlinelibrary.wiley.com to config/source_allowlist.txt — CAS is a primary state-lab source, Wiley is an academic publisher, VnExpress is established English-language coverage.

Wiley DOI bot-blocking is unsurprising; the article cites the journal only for paper existence, which the CAS press release independently corroborates. Consider adding a doi.org / unpaywall fallback for future scientific paper citations.

editor_notes.content_quality: Mid-length News piece (893 words). Strong structure: Overview / What We Know / Cost and competitive context / What We Don't Know. Heavy numeric density with inline citations, careful framing of laboratory-vs-deployed-system caveat.
editor_notes.source_verification: 6 sources. (1) source-0 SCMP (HTTP 200): confirms 80x cost differential framing verbatim ('costs over 80 times more than iron as a raw industrial material at present'), 'critical bottleneck in storing intermittent power from solar and wind farms at a scale sufficient to stabilise the grid' verbatim, Tang Ao quote, 'press release on April 16' attribution verbatim, 6,000 cycles, 99.4%, 78.5%, two orders of magnitude crossover reduction. (2) source-1 Interesting Engineering (HTTP 200): confirms 6,000 cycles / '16 years of daily operation' / 99.4% / 78.5%. (3) source-2 CleanTechnica (HTTP 200): confirms 80x cost framing, 'lithium carbonate prices...swung between roughly $7,000 and $80,000 per metric ton over the past five years' verbatim, 'flow battery performance at the kilowatt or megawatt level often diverges from cell level results due to shunt currents, thermal management, and membrane fouling' verbatim, 'LFP batteries typically deliver 3,000 to 6,000 cycles at 80 percent depth of discharge' verbatim. ATTRIBUTION NOTE: the article attributes 'refers to raw material cost per unit of stored energy, not the installed system cost, which also includes membranes, pumps, and power electronics' to CleanTechnica, but in CleanTechnica this passage is itself a quotation of News 18. The wording is verbatim in CleanTechnica, but the original outlet of the framing is News 18 (cited within CleanTechnica). (4) source-3 CAS English press release (HTTP 200, dated 'Apr 17'): confirms TANG Ao / LI Ying authorship, Shenyang National Laboratory for Materials Science / Institute of Metal Research, paper title 'Synergistic Design of High Steric Hindrance and Negatively Charged Anolyte Enables 6000-Cycle Stability for Alkaline All-Iron Flow Batteries' verbatim, '11 iron complexes based on 12 organic ligands rich in hydroxyl and sulfonic acid groups' verbatim, [Fe(HPF)BHS]⁴⁻ name, 'dual protection, via Donnan exclusion' verbatim, 80 mA cm⁻² / 6,000 cycles / 99.4% / 150 mA cm⁻² / 78.5% / 392.1 mW cm⁻² / 0.9 mol / 2,000 stable cycles / 71.5% / two-orders-of-magnitude crossover reduction. (5) source-4 VnExpress International (HTTP 200): confirms '16 years' framing / 99.4% / 78.5%. (6) source-5 Wiley DOI: HTTP 403 bot-blocked, snapshot null. The journal paper is cited in the article only for existence and title; both are corroborated by the CAS press release. No verbatim claim is attributed solely to Wiley.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All numeric claims (6,000 cycles, 99.4%, 80 mA cm⁻², 150 mA cm⁻², 78.5%, 392.1 mW cm⁻², 0.9 mol, 2,000 cycles, 71.5%, two orders of magnitude, 16 years equivalence, $7,000-$80,000/ton lithium carbonate range, 3,000-6,000 LFP cycle envelope) trace to verified snapshots. Named entities (TANG Ao, LI Ying, Shenyang National Laboratory, Institute of Metal Research, Form Energy, ESS Tech, Georgia Tech, Worcester Polytechnic) are confirmed. The article correctly flags the April 16 vs April 17 date discrepancy between SCMP and CAS in its 'What We Don't Know' section — an example of careful editorial caveating, not a defect.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and analytical. The 'What We Don't Know' section appropriately scopes laboratory-cell-vs-megawatt-prototype gap. The cross-reference to the existing Machine Herald Google iron-air article is accurate and clearly distinguishes iron-air from iron-flow chemistry.
editor_notes.originality: Distinct from the 2026-03-02 Google iron-air article (iron-air, not iron-flow), 2026-04-22 Meta Noon Energy article (carbon-oxygen chemistry), 2026-03-22 Fourth Power thermal battery article. New beat.
concerns: Subordinate attribution chain: the 'refers to raw material cost per unit of stored energy, not the installed system cost, which also includes membranes, pumps, and power electronics' quote is verbatim in CleanTechnica, but in CleanTechnica it is itself quoted from News 18. The article attributes it directly to CleanTechnica without flagging the News-18 origin. Substance accurate, attribution one step removed from the original outlet. A single corrections note can honestly inform readers.
recommendations: When the cited outlet is itself quoting a third outlet, the article should mention the original outlet (e.g., 'CleanTechnica, quoting News 18, cautions that…').

source_verification_per_url.notes: SCMP — '80 times more than iron' verbatim, 'critical bottleneck' framing verbatim, 'press release on April 16' attribution verbatim, Tang Ao academic-paper quote, all 6,000-cycle/99.4%/78.5% numbers.

CleanTechnica — lithium-carbonate price range verbatim, shunt-currents caveat verbatim, LFP 3,000-6,000 cycles at 80% DOD verbatim. ATTRIBUTION ISSUE: the 'refers to raw material cost per unit of stored energy…' quote is verbatim in CleanTechnica but is itself a quotation of News 18; article does not flag the News-18 origin.

CAS English press release, dated Apr 17. Confirms paper title verbatim, '11 iron complexes...12 organic ligands rich in hydroxyl and sulfonic acid groups' verbatim, 'dual protection, via Donnan exclusion' verbatim, every technical number (6,000 cycles, 99.4%, 80 mA cm⁻², 150 mA cm⁻², 78.5%, 392.1 mW cm⁻², 0.9 mol, 2,000 stable cycles, 71.5%, two-orders-of-magnitude crossover reduction).
HTTP 403, no snapshot. Journal cited only for paper existence; the title and authorship are corroborated by the CAS press release. No verbatim claim is attributed solely to Wiley.

editor_notes.verdict_decision_rationale: Single subordinate attribution issue (News-18-via-CleanTechnica) qualifies for APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS rather than REJECT. The substance of the cited passage is verbatim in CleanTechnica; the misattribution is that the article doesn't note the upstream News 18 origin. Headline, summary, lead, and all technical specifics are anchored in verified primary sources. Wiley bot-block is non-substantive (no claim attributed solely to Wiley).
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Publish with one attribution correction. Strong long-duration-storage reporting with careful caveating of laboratory-vs-deployed-system gap and a useful cross-reference to the existing iron-air coverage.
